suggestions.title: Stats Bar 5-Column Fixed Grid — Carry #27/#28, Third Strike
suggestions.description: App.css still reads `grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr)`. The StatsBar renders 5 base cards plus up to 2 optional cards (error count, stuck workers) for a maximum of 7 cells. When both appear, the grid silently overflows to an orphaned second row. This has been the top-priority bug for three consecutive reviews and has received ZERO code changes. Fix: one line — `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(160px, 1fr))`. The responsive breakpoints at 900px (3-col) and 600px (2-col) already exist and should be removed once auto-fill is applied.

suggestions.title: Inter Font Still Not Loaded — Carry #27/#28, Third Strike
suggestions.description: index.html contains zero font imports. The root CSS declares `font-family: 'Inter', 'Segoe UI', system-ui` but Inter is never fetched. Every Windows user silently falls back to Segoe UI; Linux users get whatever `sans-serif` resolves to. This has been open since at least review #27 and has not received a single character of fix. Fix: add `<link rel='preconnect' href='https://fonts.googleapis.com'>` and `<link href='https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;600;700;800&display=swap' rel='stylesheet'>` to index.html, OR run `npm install @fontsource/inter` and add `import '@fontsource/inter'` to main.jsx. Five minutes. That is all it takes.

suggestions.title: Mobile Office Floor: Worker Names Still Absent at ≤480px — Carry #27/#28
suggestions.description: Office.css line 1148: `.char__name { display: none }` at the ≤480px breakpoint. Workers are completely anonymous blobs on every 375px mobile device. Between 481–600px, names render at 0.44rem (~7px), which is illegible in a different direction. Three reviews, zero movement. The isometric floor was designed for desktop; the fix is to either render a simple list/card layout in place of the floor at ≤640px, or add a tap-to-reveal tooltip overlay. Continuing to ship 'names deleted' as the mobile UX is not acceptable.

suggestions.title: NEW: Fetch-Warn/Error Retry Buttons Missing isSyncing Guard — Carry #28
suggestions.description: Both `fetch-error-banner` and `fetch-warn-banner` Retry buttons call `onClick={syncFromGitHub}` with no `disabled={isSyncing}` prop. The `sync-now-btn` in the header correctly disables when isSyncing. A user mashing Retry during a slow network can fire multiple concurrent `Promise.allSettled` calls, each competing to write `setAllWorkers`, `setRoster`, and `setActivityLog`. Race condition: the last settled call wins, which may not be the most recent. One-line fix per button: add `disabled={isSyncing}` to both Retry buttons.

suggestions.title: NEW: Activity Log 'Last N Events' Header Shows Unfiltered Count
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1213: `Last {Math.min(activityLog.length, 20)} events` always reflects the total unfiltered `activityLog.length`, not the post-filter entry count. When a user activates the 'Errors' filter and sees 2 error entries, the header still says 'Last 20 events'. This is actively misleading. Fix: derive the displayed count from the filtered array. The cleanest approach: pass the filtered entries count as a prop or compute a local `filteredCount` variable before the JSX return, then render `Last {Math.min(filteredCount, 20)} events` (or `Last {filteredCount} of {activityLog.length} events` when a filter is active).

suggestions.title: NEW: Stuck Worker Stat Card Is Keyboard-Inaccessible
suggestions.description: The `stat-card--stuck` div in StatsBar (App.jsx line 453) has an `onClick` handler that scrolls to the stuck worker's card — a genuinely useful interaction — but has no `tabIndex`, no `role`, and no `onKeyDown`. Keyboard-only users cannot reach or activate it. It's a div with a click handler, invisible to tab order and screen readers. Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role='button'`, and `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); /* existing click logic */ } }}`. WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.1 (Keyboard) failure.

suggestions.title: NEW: completionToast Uses aria-live on Conditionally Rendered Element
suggestions.description: The `completionToast` div (App.jsx ~line 1060) has `aria-live='polite'` but is conditionally rendered — it does not exist in the DOM until `completionToast` state is truthy, at which point it appears with its content already set. Per the ARIA spec, an aria-live region must be present in the DOM before its content changes to reliably trigger announcements. Most screen reader + browser combos will silently ignore this. The same pattern was flagged for polling-paused-pill in review #28. Fix: render a persistent, visually-hidden `<span aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true' className='sr-only'>` that contains `completionToast ? '🎉 {completionToast.name} shipped it!' : ''`. The visible toast can remain conditional.

suggestions.title: NEW: Clickable Task Tags Have No :focus-visible Style
suggestions.description: When `onTagClick` is provided, task tag spans receive `tabIndex={0}` and `role='button'` — they are fully keyboard-interactive. But `.task-tag` has no `:focus-visible` CSS rule anywhere in App.css. The only defined focus styles are `.worker-card:focus-visible` and `.tab-toggle button:focus-visible`. Keyboard users tabbing through tags get no visible focus ring — a WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) violation. Fix: add `.task-tag:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }` to App.css.

suggestions.title: NEW: VIBE_RANK Defined Twice — Duplicate Inline Object
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 350 defines `const VIBE_RANK = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` at module scope for use in `VibeSparkline`. Line 892 defines an identical `const vibeRanking = { ... }` inline inside the vibe streak `useEffect`. These are byte-for-byte the same object. The inline one should just reference `VIBE_RANK`. DRY violation, and the duplication means the two can drift out of sync if vibe keys ever change.

suggestions.title: NEW: Worker Filter Pills Have No Active-Clear Affordance (Inconsistent with Task Tags)
suggestions.description: The worker filter pill bar in the activity log and the task tag filter system in WorkerCard both use a 'click active item to deselect' pattern, but only the task tags show a ' ×' suffix on the active tag as a visual hint that clicking will clear the filter. The worker filter pills show the same active style (purple background) but no '×', leaving users to guess whether clicking the active pill will clear or do nothing. Minor inconsistency but the precedent already exists in the codebase. Fix: apply the same `{workerFilter === member.name ? ' ×' : ''}` pattern to worker filter pill labels, matching the task tag UX.

suggestions.title: desk__nameplate--away Font Size 0.28rem (4.5px) Is Physically Unreadable
suggestions.description: Office.css line 571: `.desk__nameplate--away { font-size: 0.28rem; }`. At standard 16px base font, 0.28rem is 4.5px — below the physical resolution of most non-retina screens and the legal minimum for readable body text under any accessibility standard. The '↪ away' label is functionally invisible. Either increase to a minimum readable size (at least 0.6rem to match the base nameplate), or add `aria-hidden='true'` if it's purely decorative and rely on the away sign (`AwaySign` component) for communication. Currently it is neither readable nor properly hidden.

summary: Review #29 — score drops to 3.9 from review #28's 4.0, and that math is generous. Zero issues were fixed from the previous review. Zero. Not one. The three high-priority bugs (stats bar grid overflow, missing Inter font, mobile worker anonymity) have now collectively aged across 29 reviews like a fine wine nobody opened. Office.jsx grew another 16 lines — now 1,463 lines — while the extraction task that's been requested since review #27 continues to not happen. The foot counter modulo bug still shows wrong positions. The Escape key still doesn't clear the tag filter. The Retry buttons still fire concurrent fetches. On the new-issues front, five were found this cycle: the 'stuck worker' stat card is completely inaccessible to keyboard users (has onClick, no tabIndex, no role, no onKeyDown — textbook WCAG failure); the activity log count header misleads users when filters are active; the completion toast uses aria-live on a conditionally-rendered element (same pattern as the polling pill bug flagged last review); clickable task tags have no :focus-visible style; and VIBE_RANK is defined twice with identical values. One genuinely nice find buried in the code: `gradId` from `useId()` in Office.jsx IS used — it references a linearGradient for Luna's hair SVG. That's actually elegant SVG work. The visual craft in this codebase remains first-rate. The engineering hygiene is not keeping pace. At the current trajectory, review #30 will be auditing a 1,480-line Office.jsx against 20 unresolved findings. Ship the features? Absolutely — this app is delightful. But the backlog is a fire that keeps being called a roadmap.
